doom.driver: Trump says U.S. will 'go right back to dropping bombs' if he doesn't like Iran deal.
doom.blurb: PEACE DEAL, WITH ASTERISK THE SIZE OF A BUNKER BUSTER
doom.summary: The US-Iran 'deal' is less a ceasefire and more a strongly-worded pause, with Trump openly reserving the right to resume bombing at his discretion — which is not typically how binding international agreements work. Meanwhile Israel is still striking Lebanon despite the MOU, the Strait of Hormuz threat level was quietly 'downgraded' rather than eliminated, and BMW is issuing profit warnings from the economic blast radius. A deal that requires the dealmaker to continuously threaten the other party is called something else entirely.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: US-Iran MOU exists on paper but Trump's 'go right back to dropping bombs' framing and continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon suggest the ceasefire is structurally fragile.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Pentagon confirms 1.5 million personnel using generative AI tools for report-writing; SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60B, consolidating AI coding capability inside a defense-adjacent empire.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google joined world leaders at G7 to discuss frontier AI risks — a signal of industry power, not necessarily of meaningful oversight.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Russian warship fired warning shots near a civilian yacht in the English Channel; UK's Starmer called it 'reckless' — a minor but symbolically charged provocation in European waters.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: No major alignment incidents today; Anthropic paused token billing changes, suggesting internal prioritization of user trust over revenue extraction — faint positive signal.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — wide aerial view angled down at 45 degrees, camera looking steeply down at a vast symbolic scene rendered in deep navy and smoldering orange, the entire frame dominated by an enormous ornate mahogany table seen from above as if suspended in open air between two worlds, one half of the table surface draped in crisp cream diplomatic cloth with a thick formal document lying open at the center, signature lines visible, official seals pressed into the paper, the other half of the table scorched and crumbling away into ash and cinders, its wood blackening and dissolving into trailing smoke that drifts downward into an abyss of smoldering orange light below, the table balanced on a single fulcrum point at its center like a seesaw mid-tip, heavy shadows pooling beneath the scorched half, the cream document sliding slowly toward the burning edge, Belle Époque wrought-iron balcony railings visible at the extreme upper corners of the frame suggesting a grand lakeside palace hotel, distant Alpine snowfields catching cold blue-white light at the top of the image contrasting violently with the volcanic orange glow rising from below, smoke-filtered amber haze pressing through the entire scene, the table's reflection shimmering in smoke as though Lake Geneva itself has turned to embers beneath it.

dawn.driver: Superconductivity breakthrough could unlock ultra-efficient electronics. A clever nanoscale redesign may have solved one of superconductivity's biggest problems. Researchers in Sweden discovered that by subtly sculpting the surface beneath an ultrathin superconducting mate
dawn.blurb: SWEDISH LABS CRACK SUPERCONDUCTIVITY'S BIGGEST PUZZLE
dawn.summary: Against a backdrop of war deals and geopolitical friction, Swedish researchers quietly achieved something that could reshape how humanity powers its technology: a nanoscale fix for one of superconductivity's most stubborn engineering obstacles. If this holds, it's a step toward electronics that waste almost no energy — a profound gain for computing, medicine, and clean power infrastructure. On a day when the headlines skew heavy, a lab bench in Sweden reminds us that patient, methodical science keeps delivering.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Swedish researchers solved a core superconductivity engineering problem through nanoscale surface redesign, potentially unlocking ultra-efficient electronics at scale.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: G7 leaders signaled collective support for the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, representing rare multilateral alignment on de-escalation.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: World Cup fans from Mexico and South Korea were spotted partying together before their match, calling each other 'brother' — spontaneous human warmth across borders.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Trump administration legal battles against wind energy are faltering as clean energy output continues to surge despite political headwinds.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A daily probiotic showed measurable relief for depression and anxiety in older adults in a small clinical trial, adding a low-barrier tool to mental health care.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Weather-dominated exterior scene, thick amber-tinted fog and warm teal mist rolling through a narrow residential street in Gothenburg, Sweden, winter dusk, the atmosphere itself the dominant presence, wet cobblestones reflecting deep golden and teal light from apartment windows above. A small electronics repair shop visible through the haze, its warm amber interior glow bleeding through a fogged window, a single ceiling fan frozen mid-spin where it hangs from the ceiling, power tools resting idle on a workbench visible through the glass. On the wet street outside, a bicycle leans against a lamp post, its small dynamo headlight glowing impossibly bright and steady despite no rider, a faint ring of golden light around the bulb suggesting effortless, unbroken current. The fog softens every edge, halos every light source into radiant bloom, teal shadows pooling in doorways and under eaves while amber warmth pours from the shop window. A power line overhead vanishes into the mist, its cable perfectly taut and dry against the wet grey-blue winter sky, suggesting clean transmission through the murk. Deep golden hour amber and warm teal throughout, side-lighting casting long soft shadows across wet stone, radiant and still.
